title: Week 1: Aarav Builds Wallet Onboarding
paragraphs: Aarav joined the Stellar Journey program as a frontend developer who had never shipped an on-chain flow before. His first goal was simple: make onboarding feel less scary for new users who only know Web2 apps. He noticed that even technically curious users left during wallet setup because too many unknown terms appeared too early.

The target project is a guided wallet onboarding sequence for students joining local blockchain communities. Instead of dropping users into a dashboard, Aarav designed a path with clear single-action steps: create wallet, confirm phrase handling, connect account, and make one tiny test transaction. He intentionally moved advanced settings out of the first screen so the flow feels focused.

His reason for solving this is personal. Aarav almost quit his first chain project because setup felt like reading documentation rather than using a product. He wants first-time builders to feel momentum in the first ten minutes, not pressure. That is why he treats onboarding as product design, not just form validation.

He builds in short cycles: map one step, prototype one screen, test with two peers, then improve based on hesitation points. Outside coding, he is balancing final semester exams and part-time tutoring. To stay consistent, he commits to two focused hours daily and ships one visible improvement every three days.
